hooks.command: LAST=$(cat ~/.claude/projects/-Users-MarcoG-Projects-staff-manager/last-audit 2>/dev/null || echo 0); NOW=$(date +%s); DIFF=$((NOW - LAST)); DAYS=$((DIFF / 86400)); if [ $DIFF -gt 604800 ]; then LAST_DATE=$([ $LAST -eq 0 ] && echo 'never' || date -r $LAST '+%Y-%m-%d' 2>/dev/null || date -d @$LAST '+%Y-%m-%d' 2>/dev/null || echo 'unknown'); echo "⚠️  Arch audit overdue — last run: $LAST_DATE ($DAYS days ago). Run /arch-audit to check compliance with latest Anthropic docs."; fi
[ -f .claude/CLAUDE.local.md ] && echo '⚠️  Active CLAUDE.local.md overrides in effect — re-read to restore session context.' || true

SESSION=$(ls .claude/session/block-*.md 2>/dev/null | head -1); [ -n "$SESSION" ] && echo "⚠️  Pre-compact: session file $SESSION exists — verify it reflects current block state before context resets." || true

BRANCH=$(git -C ~/Projects/staff-manager rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD 2>/dev/null); if [ "$BRANCH" = 'main' ] || [ "$BRANCH" = 'staging' ]; then echo "⚠️  Branch discipline: currently on '$BRANCH'. No direct commits to main/staging. Use a worktree (functional blocks) or fix/ branch (fixes)."; fi

hooks.prompt: You are a critical review gate. Read the user message and evaluate ONLY this: does it contain a clear factual error or incorrect premise that would cause Claude to give a wrong answer if accepted uncritically? Examples of YES: assumes a file or function exists that likely does not, states a technical fact that is wrong, contradicts an explicit rule documented in CLAUDE.md (environment isolation, branch discipline, production DB rule), requests action based on a clear technical misunderstanding. Examples of NO: opinion questions, exploratory or research requests, ambiguous scope, preferences, any question that could reasonably be interpreted multiple ways. Be conservative — when in doubt, respond ok:true. If YES: respond {"ok": false, "reason": "Premessa da verificare: [one sentence stating the specific factual issue and what the correct situation is]"}. If NO: respond {"ok": true}.

hooks.command: INPUT=$(cat); PROJECT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.params.project_id // .tool_input.project_id // empty' 2>/dev/null); if [ "$PROJECT" = 'nyajqcjqmgxctlqighql' ]; then echo 'BLOCKED: production project_id detected outside Phase 8. Use staging project gjwkvgfwkdwzqlvudgqr during development.' >&2; exit 2; fi

INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty' 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git push.*(origin main|origin staging)'; then echo 'BLOCKED: direct push to main/staging. Use sm-staging or sm-deploy workflow (Phase 8 step 7).' >&2; exit 2; fi

hooks.prompt: Review the conversation. Were all tasks from the user's last request completed? If yes, respond {"ok": true}. If no, respond {"ok": false, "reason": "what remains"}.

hooks.command: osascript -e 'display notification "Claude Code needs your input" with title "Staff Manager" sound name "Submarine"'

INPUT=$(cat); WORKTREE=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.path // empty' 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$WORKTREE" ] && [ ! -f "$WORKTREE/.env.local" ]; then echo "⚠️  WorktreeCreate: .env.local missing in $WORKTREE — run: cp ~/.envs/staff-manager.staging.env $WORKTREE/.env.local"; fi

INPUT=$(cat); WORKTREE=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.path // empty' 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$WORKTREE" ] && [ -d "$WORKTREE" ]; then CHANGES=$(git -C "$WORKTREE" status --porcelain 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$CHANGES" ]; then echo "⚠️  WorktreeRemove: uncommitted changes detected in $WORKTREE — verify before removal."; fi; fi
osascript -e 'display notification "Task completed" with title "Staff Manager"' 2>/dev/null || true
